heading: Estimates of precipitation frequency in the United States
label: 9707 Estimates of precipitation frequency in the United States

children.heading: Freely Associated States
children.label: 1 Freely Associated States
children.text: The term “Freely Associated States” means the Republic of Palau, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and the Federated States of Micronesia, which have each entered into a Compact of Free Association with the United States.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 2 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means the 50 States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Freely Associated States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: b In general
children.text: The Administrator shall establish a program, to be known as the “NOAA Precipitation Frequency Atlas of the United States”, to compile, estimate, analyze, and communicate the frequency of precipitation in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: temporal and spatial distribution of heavy precipitation;

children.label: B
children.text: analyses of seasonality in precipitation; and

children.label: C
children.text: trends in annual maximum series data; and
may serve as the official source of the Federal Government on estimates of precipitation frequency and associated information with respect to the United States.

children.heading: Coverage
children.label: 1 Coverage
children.text: The NOAA Precipitation Frequency Atlas of the United States shall include such estimates of the frequency of precipitation in the United States as the Administrator determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be conducted not less frequently than once every 10 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: may be conducted more frequently if determined appropriate by the Administrator.

children.label: A
children.text: subject to an appropriate, scientific process, as determined by the Administrator; and

children.label: B
children.text: published on a publicly accessible website of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

children.heading: Partnerships
children.label: e Partnerships
children.text: The Administrator may partner with other Federal agencies, members of the private sector, academic cooperative partnerships, or nongovernment associations to assist in carrying out the functions described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: f Consultation
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Administrator may consult with relevant Federal, State, local, Tribal, and Territorial government agencies, research institutions, and the private sector, as the Administrator determines necessary.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: g Coordination
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Administrator may coordinate with other Federal agencies.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section, from amounts otherwise authorized to be appropriated to the Administrator to carry out this chapter, $3,500,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 through 2030.
